playbook.intent: Failed launch → clean shutdown plus a sharper next-bet plan in 30 days.
playbook.scope: Turn a missed launch into a clean shutdown plus a sharper next-bet plan: refund/migrate users, harvest learnings publicly, recycle assets, and decide on rebuild vs pivot vs walk away with an explicit rationale.

success-criteria.criterion: Documented postmortem with evidence and 3 lessons
All paying users either refunded or migrated
Assets harvested into a reusable library
Written decision: rebuild / pivot / walk away
Next-bet brief or final retirement note

stage.name: Assess
stage.intent: Make the failure crisp with numbers and quotes.

tasks.task: Re-run the success metrics against the actual launch
Collect 5 user quotes on why the product missed
Distinguish problem-fit failure vs distribution failure

stage.decision-gate: Advance when failure mode is named in one sentence.

stage.name: Sunset Comms
stage.intent: Users hear it from you first, with a clear path.

tasks.task: Send a sunset email with refund or migration path
Publish a public retro on IH and X

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after all paying users have a confirmed status.

stage.name: Asset Harvest
stage.intent: Code, copy, audience, and infra survive even if the product does not.

tasks.task: Tag reusable components, prompts, and copy
Migrate audience to an umbrella newsletter or handle
Cut infra costs — pull the plug on unused services

stage.decision-gate: Advance once monthly infra costs are documented at the new baseline.

stage.name: Lessons
stage.intent: A public retro that builds trust and refines the next bet.

tasks.task: Write a 3-lesson postmortem ranked by stakes
Frame the lessons in MoM-test language (no bragging)
Invite reader replies — feed the next-bet idea pool

stage.decision-gate: Advance only once the retro is public and triaged.

stage.name: Next Bet
stage.intent: Choose rebuild, pivot, or retire with a documented rationale.

tasks.task: Score 3 candidate next bets using a quick RICE
Run niche-evaluation on the top score
Write the decision: rebuild / pivot / walk away

stage.decision-gate: Required output: explicit verdict. Pivot only with a fresh JTBD; walking away is valid.

extra.value: [Reviving the failed product for vanity reasons, Long-form sunk-cost rescue attempts, Hiring help to keep the product alive — explicit kill option]
[A launched but underperforming product, A baseline metric showing the launch fell short]
[Treating the playbook as a checklist instead of a decision tree, Skipping decision gates when intermediate progress feels good, Letting tools or platforms replace the founder's own judgement]

['Did each stage produce its named output or get explicitly skipped with a note?', 'Were decision gates respected or quietly bypassed?', 'Is the evidence trail strong enough to defend the verdict externally?']

{human: faion get-content sunset-and-rebuild-from-failed-launch --format md, agent: faion get-content sunset-and-rebuild-from-failed-launch --format context, json: faion get-content sunset-and-rebuild-from-failed-launch --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft from brainstorm-2026-05-17, persona P2 (indie hacker)'}]
